dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, {target_npc} found out. They know it was me who ran.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Someone recognized me in the market and told {target_npc} everything. They came to my door, shaking with anger, and demanded to know why. I couldn't lie anymore: I told them the truth. They didn't hit me, didn't shout. They just said, 'Then prove you've changed.' They want me to help defend {location} personally. But first, we need more {quest_item} from {location_hint}, and the {enemy_type} are thicker than ever. This time, I can't run.
dialogueChunks.outro: I asked for a chance to make amends and I got one. The hard kind. Will you stand with me?

playerResponses.text: You asked for this. Now earn it. I'll stand with you.
Proving yourself under fire. That's the only language that matters.
Your redemption arc isn't my problem.

dialogueChunks.intro: The medicine we gathered, {player_name}... it's been stolen.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: Bandits raided the supply wagon {location_hint} before it reached {location}. Everything: every vial, every herb, every ounce of {quest_item}. {target_npc} doesn't even know the shipment was coming from me. They just know that the settlement's medicine supply is dangerously low again. If I don't replace it quickly, more people will die, and this time I can't hide behind anonymity. The bandits left a trail through {enemy_type} territory, and the supplies might still be recoverable.
dialogueChunks.outro: The universe has a cruel sense of humor. But I'm not laughing, and I'm not quitting.

playerResponses.text: Then we get it back. Every last drop.
Stolen medicine in enemy territory. Your redemption just got expensive.
Fate seems to be telling you something. Maybe listen.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}... {target_npc} came to the memorial site while I was building it.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: He saw the half-finished monument to his daughter and broke down. I tried to leave before he noticed me, but he caught my arm and asked why a stranger was honoring his child. I couldn't speak. He read it on my face, {player_name}. He read the truth right off me like words on a page. He didn't say anything: just walked away. Now the site has been wrecked. {enemy_type} tore through it last night, scattered the {quest_item} I'd already placed. I don't know if {target_npc} will ever speak to me again, but I have to finish what I started. For her, not for me.
dialogueChunks.outro: The memorial doesn't need to be for my peace. It needs to exist because she deserved to be remembered.

playerResponses.text: For her, then. Let's rebuild it right.
You owe him the truth in words, not just stones. But I'll help with the stones.
Some wounds heal. Some don't. I can't tell you which this is.
